problem: In a small town, there are three water tanks: A, B, and C. Tank A can hold 2000 liters of water, Tank B can hold 3000 liters, and Tank C can hold 2500 liters. The town has two water pumps: Pump 1 and Pump 2. Pump 1 can fill Tank A at a rate of 100 liters per minute and Tank B at a rate of 150 liters per minute. Pump 2 can fill Tank B at a rate of 200 liters per minute and Tank C at a rate of 120 liters per minute. If Tank A is already filled to 1500 liters, Tank B is filled to 1000 liters, and Tank C is empty, how many minutes will it take to fill all three tanks if both pumps are used simultaneously and Pump 1 is used for Tank A and Pump 2 is used for Tank C, but they switch to Tank B after Tank A and Tank C are full?
reasoning_steps: Step 1: Calculate the remaining capacity for each tank. Tank A needs 2000 - 1500 = 500 liters, Tank B needs 3000 - 1000 = 2000 liters, and Tank C needs 2500 liters.

Step 2: Determine how long it will take to fill Tank A and Tank C with their respective pumps. For Tank A, it will take 500 / 100 = 5 minutes to fill with Pump 1. For Tank C, it will take 2500 / 120 = 20.83 minutes to fill with Pump 2.

Step 3: After Tank A is full, Pump 1 will switch to Tank B. By the time Tank A is full, 5 minutes have passed, and in those 5 minutes, Pump 2 has filled 5 * 120 = 600 liters of Tank C, leaving 2500 - 600 = 1900 liters in Tank C.

Step 4: Calculate how long it will take to fill the remaining capacity of Tank C and Tank B. Tank C will take 1900 / 120 = 15.83 minutes to fill with Pump 2. Tank B will be filled by both Pump 1 and Pump 2 after Tank A and initially by Pump 2 before Tank C is full. First, calculate how much of Tank B is filled by Pump 2 before Tank C is full.

Step 5: Calculate the combined rate of filling Tank B after Tank A is full and before Tank C is full. Pump 1 fills Tank B at 150 liters per minute, and Pump 2 fills it at 200 liters per minute, for a combined rate of 350 liters per minute. Since Tank C takes 20.83 minutes to fill and Tank A takes 5 minutes, the time when both pumps are filling Tank B is after 5 minutes and before 20.83 minutes, which is 15.83 minutes. But since Pump 1 starts filling Tank B after 5 minutes, it fills Tank B for the remaining time until Tank C is full, which is 15.83 minutes. In this time, Pump 1 fills 15.83 * 150 = 2374.5 liters of Tank B, but since Tank B only needs 2000 liters, it will be full before Tank C is full.
final_answer: It will take 20.83 minutes to fill Tank C, which is the longest time among all tanks, and by this time, Tank A and Tank B will also be full because Tank B is filled at a faster rate than Tank C.
